architecture.description: Q-Ising is a 3-stage pipeline: (1) Bayesian Dynamic Ising Model for network state estimation, (2) Offline Q-Learning (CQL) over latent states, (3) Ensemble Policy via MCMC posterior draws.

components.name: DynamicIsingModel
components.role: Stage 1 — estimate node-level adoption probabilities from panel data
components.input: Panel D = {y0, (a_t, y_t)_{t=1}^{T_train}}, adjacency M, node features X, bin partition {B_1..B_K}
components.output: Estimated parameters theta_hat per node/bin; latent adoption probabilities l_hat^0_{i,t}
components.operation: Node-wise logistic pseudo-likelihood with spike-and-slab priors; estimation via EMVS or HMC

components.name: StateConstructor
components.role: Stage 1 (post-inference) — aggregate node-level latent states to bin-level Q-Ising states
components.input: Estimated no-intervention adoption probabilities l_hat^0_{i,t} and observed outcomes y_{t-1}
components.operation: Bin-level averaging; counterfactual state set by zeroing treatment indicators (a_t = empty)

components.name: OfflineCQL
components.role: Stage 2 — learn Q-function over bin-level state-action space from offline transitions
components.input: Offline transitions D_RL = {(s_t, b_t, r_t, s_{t+1})}
components.output: Q-function Q: S x [K] -> R; greedy policy pi_hat(s) = argmax_b Q(s,b)
components.operation: Conservative Q-Learning (CQL) with Bellman error + conservative penalty (alpha=0.1); neural network Q-function

components.name: EnsemblePolicyMCMC
components.role: Stage 3 — uncertainty quantification via posterior sampling
components.output: Ensemble policy pi_hat^ens(s) = argmax_k sum_p 1{pi_hat^(p)(s) = k} (majority vote)
components.operation: Train P separate CQL agents on P different state representations; majority-vote aggregation

variants.name: EMVS variant
variants.description: Point-estimate using EMVS for fast MAP inference; single CQL agent

variants.name: Ensemble/MCMC variant
variants.description: Full posterior via HMC; P=10-20 CQL agents trained; majority-vote policy

ambiguities.description: Neural network architecture for Q-function not specified (depth, width, activation). Only hidden layers [256,256] mentioned in Appendix E.
EMVS convergence criterion not fully specified; '2-3 steps' mentioned but not exact stopping rule.

equations.name: Dynamic Ising Linear Predictor
equations.note: k = bin of node i; m_j = bin of neighbor j; N_i = neighbors of i

equations.name: Spike-and-Slab Prior for coupling parameters
equations.latex: \gamma_{i,j} | z_{ij} \sim (1-z_{ij}) N(0, v_0) + z_{ij} N(0, v_1), \quad z_{ij} \sim \text{Bernoulli}\left(\frac{c}{|B_{m_i}|}\right)
equations.note: For baseline/treatment/persistence: beta_{l,k} ~ N(0, tau^2) with tau^2=10.0

equations.name: No-intervention latent adoption probability
equations.note: a_t=empty means treatment indicators set to zero

tensors.name: y_t
tensors.role: observed adoption outcomes at period t

tensors.name: eta_it
tensors.role: linear predictor for node i at t

tensors.name: l_hat_0_it
tensors.role: counterfactual no-treatment adoption probability for node i

tensors.name: l_bar_0_t
tensors.role: bin-averaged counterfactual probabilities (forward-looking component of state)

tensors.name: y_bar_t
tensors.role: bin-averaged observed outcomes (backward-looking component of state)

tensors.name: s_t
tensors.role: Q-Ising state vector; concatenation of l_bar and y_bar

tensors.name: Q(s,b)
tensors.role: Q-function output for all bin actions

tensors.name: theta_i
tensors.role: per-node Ising parameter vector (intercept + treatment + persistence + spillover + couplings)

tensor_semantics.note: N = number of nodes, K = number of bins. State dimension is 2K. Q-network input dim = 2K, output dim = K.

stage1_ising.optimizer: EMVS (EM-based variational selection) for MAP; HMC for full posterior
stage1_ising.library_hmc: PyMC or Stan (not specified; assumed PyMC given Python ecosystem)

priors.coupling_gamma: Spike-and-slab: spike N(0, 0.01), slab N(0, 10.0), inclusion prob c/|B_m| with c=1.0

stage1_ising.wall_clock: approx 20-30 seconds for state construction; 1 minute for ensemble with 200 MCMC draws

training_pipeline.hardware: Apple M1 chip (CPU only; no GPU specified)
training_pipeline.reproducibility: All experiments seeded for reproducibility (exact seed values not stated)

metrics.name: Mean adoption rate per period
metrics.aggregation: averaged over test horizon H and n_test_runs

baselines.name: Degree
baselines.description: Degree centrality — target highest degree node

baselines.name: LIR
baselines.description: Local influence ranking (Liu et al. 2017) — identifies local degree leaders

baselines.name: Degree-Bin
baselines.description: Topology heuristic that iterates bins selecting highest degree untreated node

baselines.name: Plain DQN
baselines.description: Offline RL (same CQL framework) without Ising state augmentation — uses raw observations only

primary_results_sbm.note: Q-Ising achieves highest adoption rate and fastest ramp in SBM. Random outperforms pure topology methods because it seeds susceptible blocks by chance.

primary_results_villages.note: Q-Ising matches or improves over Degree-Bin in majority of 42 villages. Performance gain negatively correlated with village modularity (approx -0.5). Full table in paper Table 2.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: PyTorch used as deep learning backend for CQL network
PyMC3 or NumPyro used for HMC sampling
implementation_assumptions.basis: Common Python Bayesian libraries; paper cites Hoffman & Gelman 2014 (NUTS/HMC)

implementation_assumptions.assumption: igraph Python library for edge-betweenness community detection
Community assignment (bin assignment) done once at initialization using spectral or edge-betweenness clustering, then held fixed
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper describes bins as fixed partition; edge-betweenness is stated

implementation_assumptions.assumption: EMVS inner loop uses sklearn LogisticRegression with L1/L2 penalty weighted by spike-slab posterior inclusion probabilities
implementation_assumptions.basis: EMVS (Rockova & George 2014) description; convex optimization per iteration

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Node-level Ising model is estimated independently per node (node-wise likelihood factorization)
Random seeds for experiments not explicitly given; reproducibility must be ensured by setting global seeds
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper says 'seeded appropriately' without specifying values

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Q-network uses ReLU activations between hidden layers
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard in CQL/d3rlpy; not specified in paper

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Bin-level transitions are mapped from node-level by simply identifying which bin the treated node belongs to
For the SBM experiment, K=4 bins correspond exactly to the 4 ground-truth blocks
implementation_assumptions.basis: Figure 1 caption states K=4 bins; 4-block SBM

ambiguities.description: EMVS implementation details: exact inner optimization solver not specified
HMC library not named. Hoffman & Gelman 2014 (NUTS) is cited, suggesting NUTS variant of HMC
How bins are defined for SBM experiment is not explicitly stated — likely matching to ground-truth communities
ambiguities.primary_assumption: K=4 bins = 4 SBM blocks (identified by spectral clustering)
